Team News: Ivory Coast vs. Ecuador injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Philadelphia Stadium

# Team News: Ivory Coast vs. Ecuador injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 14 Jun 2026 00:00

© Iconsport / GSI

Three-time African champions Ivory Coast face Ecuador in what promises to be a physical 2026 World Cup group-stage clash in Philadelphia on Sunday.

The Elephants head into the encounter on a four-match winning run, while the defensively resolute South Americans are unbeaten over the past two years.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up all the latest team news for both nations.

---

## IVORY COAST vs. ECUADOR

## IVORY COAST

Out: Evan Ndicka (thigh)

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Y. Fofana; Doue, Agbadou, Kossounou, Konan; Kessie, Sangare, S. Fofana; Diallo, Guessand, Diomande

## ECUADOR

Out: None

Doubtful: Enner Valencia(calf)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Galin

news_items.title: Preview: Ivory Coast vs. Ecuador - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Preview: Ivory Coast vs. Ecuador - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Philadelphia Stadium

# Preview: Ivory Coast vs. Ecuador - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 12 Jun 2026 12:21, Last updated: 13 Jun 2026 22:59

© Iconsport / Ewen Gavet

Ecuador and Ivory Coast face off at Lincoln Financial Field in one of the standout fixtures of the 2026 World Cup opening round.

While Germany are tipped to top Group E, the three-time African champions and La Tri will certainly have other ideas, meaning Sunday's fixture could lay down a crucial marker.

---

## Match preview

When Ivory Coast were defeated by Greece in June 2014, few Elephants supporters would have believed that the clash in Fortaleza would be their last at a global finals for over a decade.

Instead, the three-time African champions missed out on Russia four years later and were absent from the thrilling 2022 tournament in Qatar.

Sunday night marks the Ivorian side's return to the global stage 12 years after that heartbreaking Greece fixture, with Emerse Fae's side looking to break new ground.

Featured Betting Offer

50/1 on Spain to qu

news_items.title: Ivory Coast vs Ecuador – Predicted lineup and team news - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: The Football Faithful Sun, June 14, 2026 at 4:55 a.m. UTC · 2 min read
news_items.text:
Ivory Coast vs Ecuador – Predicted lineup and team news - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Story by

Ivory Coast vs Ecuador – Predicted lineup and team news

Ivory Coast will take on Ecuador in their first Group E match of the FIFA World Cup, set to be played at Philadelphia Stadium on Monday.

The Elephants come into the tournament in excellent form, having won their last three friendly matches. Their standout performance was an impressive 2-1 win against France.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Before that, they secured a dominant 4-0 victory over South Korea and edged past Scotland with a narrow 1-0 win. Their most recent loss dates back to January, when they were beaten 3-2 by Egypt in the quarterfinals of the Africa Cup of Nations.

## Ivory Coast team news

Ivory Coast faces some selection concerns ahead of this opening game at Lincoln Financial Field. Head coach Emerse Fae will have to cope without Roma defender Evan Ndicka, who has been ruled out due to an ongoing thigh injury.

Advertisement

Advertisement

On a positive note, Odilon Kossounou has recovered from a recent injury and is expected to start in defence alongside Emmanuel Ag

news_items.title: World Cup 2026: Côte d'Ivoire records their first absence - Foot Africa
news_items.text:
World Cup 2026: Côte d'Ivoire records their first absence - Foot Africa

# World Cup 2026: Côte d'Ivoire records their first absence

Clement Akpa of AJ Auxerre ruled out due to injury

Africa 30 May 2026 at 02:03

World Cup 2026: Côte d'Ivoire records their first absence

Bad news for Côte d'Ivoire: Clement Akpa, who was called up by Emerse Faé for the 2026 World Cup squad, has announced his withdrawal. According to the Ivorian Football Federation, the player has suffered an injury.

A cruel blow for the Elephants and for the young Clement Akpa, who will miss out on what would have been his World Cup debut in the 2026 World Cup. The young left-back from AJ Auxerre picked up the injury and will miss out on the biggest football tournament on the planet.

🚨 Official statementWorld Cup 2026Clement Akpa out, Christopher Operi called upThe Ivorian Football Federation announces to the media, partners, and sporting public that international Clement Akpa, who was called up by national coach Emerse Faé for the squad... pic.twitter.com/ZnvGNuEej9

— National team of Côte d'Ivoire 🇨🇮🐘 (@equipenatciv) May 29, 2026

The source is the Ivorian Football Federation. To fill the gap, Emerse Faé

# Preview: Ivory Coast vs. Ecuador - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 12 Jun 2026 12:21, Last updated: 13 Jun 2026 22:59

© Iconsport / Ewen Gavet

Ecuador and Ivory Coast face off at Lincoln Financial Field in one of the standout fixtures of the 2026 World Cup opening round.

While Germany are tipped to top Group E, the three-time African champions and La Tri will certainly have other ideas, meaning Sunday's fixture could lay down a crucial marker.

---

## Match preview

When Ivory Coast were defeated by Greece in June 2014, few Elephants supporters would have believed that the clash in Fortaleza would be their last at a global finals for over a decade.

Instead, the three-time African champions missed out on Russia four years later and were absent from the thrilling 2022 tournament in Qatar.

Sunday night marks the Ivorian side's return to the global stage 12 years after that heartbreaking Greece fixture, with Emerse Fae's side looking to break new ground.

Ivory Coast vs Ecuador – Predicted lineup and team news

Ivory Coast will take on Ecuador in their first Group E match of the FIFA World Cup, set to be played at Philadelphia Stadium on Monday.

The Elephants come into the tournament in excellent form, having won their last three friendly matches. Their standout performance was an impressive 2-1 win against France.

Ivory Coast faces some selection concerns ahead of this opening game at Lincoln Financial Field. Head coach Emerse Fae will have to cope without Roma defender Evan Ndicka, who has been ruled out due to an ongoing thigh injury.
